claim: Boris Cherny (Claude Code creator) starts 80% of sessions in Plan Mode; a good plan locks execution on track with minimal iteration
actionable: Always start in Plan Mode; iterate the plan until satisfied before building; move slow to move fast
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: Claude.md files should be minimal (a few thousand tokens); overly long Claude.md files confuse models and prevent application of critical rules
actionable: Keep Claude.md short and focused; periodically delete and rebuild from scratch or trim unnecessary rules
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: Model capabilities improve daily; instructions that worked 6 months ago are likely built into the model now; do the minimal necessary thing and add back only on failure
actionable: Trim Claude.md when models update; don't assume old instructions remain necessary; add back only when you see repeated failures
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: Giving Claude a verification feedback loop (way to check its own work) provides a 2-3x multiplier on output quality
actionable: Provide tools for self-verification (browser testing, test running, linting); ask Claude to verify its work before you accept it
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: Multiple parallel Claude Code sessions working on different, non-overlapping tasks outperform sequential sessions; fresh context prevents cognitive baggage
actionable: Use multiple parallel Claude Code sessions for independent tasks; fresh context catches what familiar contexts miss
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: Slash commands and Claude Skills systematize inner-loop workflows (repeated tasks throughout the day); documenting once makes them infinitely repeatable
actionable: Identify inner-loop tasks; create Claude Skills or slash commands for them; reuse instead of re-prompting
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: The bitter lesson: more general models always beat more specific models; never bet against model improvement; avoid micro-tweaks that will become unnecessary
actionable: Focus on information architecture (context, skills, system prompts) over prompt optimization; models improve constantly, making tweaks obsolete
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project

claim: AI will never be as bad as it is today; spend energy on information architecture and tooling, not on prompt optimization
actionable: Build scalable information structures; invest in agent architecture, not brittle prompt engineering
source_title: How Claude Code's Creator Starts EVERY Project
